skill.name: Azure Bot Service Management SDK for Python
skill.description: Manage Azure Bot Service resources including bots, channels, and OAuth connections using the Azure Management SDK for Python.
skill.summary: Create, configure, and manage Azure Bot Service resources with Python SDK for conversational AI deployments.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation-only skill providing instructional content for Azure Bot Service Management SDK. Static analysis found 0 files with executable code and 0 security issues. The skill teaches legitimate cloud resource management patterns using official Azure SDK. Environment variable usage shown is for standard Azure authentication configuration.

content.user_title: Manage Azure Bot Service with Python SDK
content.value_statement: Deploying and managing conversational AI bots on Azure requires complex configuration across multiple services. This skill streamlines bot creation, channel setup, and OAuth connections using the official Azure Management SDK for Python.
actual_capabilities: Create and configure Azure Bot Service resources with specified SKU tiers
Manage bot channels including Teams, Direct Line, Web Chat, and Slack
Configure OAuth connection settings for user authentication
List and retrieve bot configurations across resource groups
Update bot properties and delete bot resources
Retrieve channel keys and connection settings
limitations: Requires Azure subscription and appropriate IAM permissions
Does not handle bot application code or hosting infrastructure
Channel configuration requires separate service registrations

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer Deploying Bots
use_cases.description: Automate bot resource provisioning and channel configuration as part of CI/CD pipelines using Python scripts.
use_cases.target_user: DevOps engineers managing conversational AI infrastructure

use_cases.title: Developer Testing Bot Integrations
use_cases.description: Quickly create test bot instances with various channel configurations for development and testing workflows.

use_cases.title: Cloud Administrator Managing Bot Fleet
use_cases.description: Inventory and manage multiple bot services across subscriptions with consistent configuration policies.
use_cases.target_user: Cloud administrators overseeing Azure Bot Service deployments

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Bot
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Azure Bot Service resource named 'customer-support-bot' in the free tier with endpoint 'https://myapp.azurewebsites.net/api/messages'. Use the resource group 'bot-resources' and display name 'Customer Support Assistant'.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: Deploying your first bot with basic configuration

prompt_templates.title: Configure Multiple Channels
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up Direct Line and Web Chat channels for the bot 'sales-assistant-bot'. Enable the Direct Line site for v3 API and create a default Web Chat site.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: Enabling multiple communication channels for a bot

prompt_templates.title: Configure OAuth for User Authentication
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an OAuth connection named 'graph-api' for bot 'hr-assistant' with scopes for User.Read and Calendar.Read. Use the service provider ID for Microsoft Graph integration.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: Setting up user authentication for accessing Microsoft Graph

prompt_templates.title: Audit Bot Resources Across Subscription
prompt_templates.prompt: List all Azure Bot Service resources in my subscription and show which resource groups contain bots. For each bot, display its name, SKU tier, and configured channels.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: Inventory and audit of bot resources for compliance reporting

output_examples.input: Create a new Azure Bot in resource group 'demo-rg' with F0 SKU
output_examples.output:
Bot created: demo-chat-bot
Location: global
SKU: F0 (Free tier)
Endpoint configured: https://demo-chat-bot.azurewebsites.net/api/messages

Configured channels:
- DirectLineChannel: Enabled (v3 API)
- WebChatChannel: Enabled (Default Site)
- MsTeamsChannel: Enabled

Connection: graph-api
  Provider: Microsoft Graph
  Scopes: User.Read, Calendar.Read
  Status: Active

best_practices: Use DefaultAzureCredential for authentication and store secrets in Azure Key Vault
Start with F0 SKU for development and upgrade to S1 for production workloads
Enable only required channels to minimize security exposure and manage complexity
anti_patterns: Do not embed client secrets or connection keys directly in code or configuration files
Avoid enabling all available channels without evaluating security and compliance requirements
Do not skip rotating Direct Line channel keys on a regular schedule

faq.question: What Azure subscription permissions are required to use this skill?
faq.answer: You need Contributor or Bot Service Contributor role on the target resource group, plus registration permissions for the Microsoft.BotService resource provider.

faq.question: Can I use this skill to migrate bots between subscriptions?
faq.answer: This skill manages individual bot resources but does not support direct migration. Export bot configuration and recreate in the target subscription.

faq.question: What is the difference between F0 and S1 SKU tiers?
faq.answer: F0 is free with message rate limits suitable for development. S1 supports unlimited messages and production workloads with higher scale limits.

faq.question: How do I configure my bot for Microsoft Teams deployment?
faq.answer: Use the MsTeamsChannel configuration to enable Teams integration. You must also register the bot with the Teams manifest and deploy through Teams Admin Center.

faq.question: What authentication method should I use for automation scripts?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential which supports multiple auth methods including managed identity, environment credentials, and Azure CLI authentication.

faq.question: How often should I rotate Direct Line channel keys?
faq.answer: Rotate Direct Line keys every 90 days or when personnel with access change. Generate new keys before disabling old ones to avoid service disruption.
